sourceMovie.title: Moby Dick
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Moby Dick works as a adventure movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The adventure and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Life of Pi
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Moby Dick clicked for you, Life of Pi (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Moby Dick?
faq.answer: Try Life of Pi, In the Heart of the Sea, Master and Commander: The Far Side of the World—each shares DNA with Moby Dick while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Moby Dick?
faq.answer: Moby Dick (1956) is best described as Adventure, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Moby Dick worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this adventure lane.
